title: Using Market Mechanisms to Advance Paris Agreement
summary: The Harvard Project on Climate Agreements has released a volume of 17 briefs summarizing research on options for implementing Article 6, the evolution of market-based systems, and how these two processes might best support each other.
body: To mitigate climate change and its impacts, the Paris Agreement of 2015 offers participating countries the opportunity to cooperate with one another when implementing their required national emission reduction targets. The mechanisms designed to assist this process are intended to make it easier to achieve agreed-to reduction targets. The cooperation mechanisms described in Article 6 of the Paris Agreement form the legal framework to allow use of market-based climate change mitigation mechanisms. The Harvard Project on Climate Agreements has released a volume of 17 briefs summarizing research on options for implementing Article 6, the evolution of market-based systems, and how these two processes might best support each other. The briefs are designed for negotiators, policymakers, and other stakeholders, as they consider how to carry out the Paris Agreement in order to realize its potential to effectively address global climate change. Read the full briefs here . The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "Using Market Mechanisms to Advance Paris Agreement." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School, Fall/Winter 2017-2018. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Paris Agreement of 2015 provides a framework for international cooperation to help countries meet their national emission reduction targets. Article 6 of the agreement establishes the legal basis for using market-based mechanisms to facilitate this cooperation and make achieving climate goals more cost-effective. The Harvard Project on Climate Agreements has compiled a volume of 17 research briefs examining the implementation of Article 6, the evolution of carbon markets, and how these two areas can synergistically support the Agreement's objectives. This research is intended as a resource for negotiators, policymakers, and stakeholders as they work to operationalize the Paris Agreement and maximize its potential to effectively address global climate change.
result.main_content_zh: 2015年通过的《巴黎协定》为各国合作实现国家自主减排目标提供了框架。协定第六条建立了利用市场机制促进此类合作的法律基础，旨在降低实现气候目标的成本。哈佛气候协议项目发布了一份包含17篇研究简报的文集，探讨了第六条的实施、碳市场体系的演变以及两者如何协同支持《巴黎协定》的目标。这些研究简报旨在为谈判代表、政策制定者及其他利益相关方提供参考，帮助他们落实《巴黎协定》，并充分发挥其应对全球气候变化的潜力。
